amendment_scope: Restore causal exchange event time to the compact event link and close terminal-report failure handling. Exact wire, typed-event, market, feature, target, and model semantics remain unchanged.

failure_evidence.failure: The v3 post-capture latency query expected event_time_ms in the depth typed table. Replay passed, but no terminal report or qualification was produced.

inheritance.rule: All v1 through v3 logical requirements remain binding except that v4 replaces the v3 event-link table and terminal failure classification below.

event_link_v4.event_time_semantics: Provider exchange event time exactly as parsed from the already persisted exact wire receipt; NULL only when that event type has no provider event time or rejected wire cannot support one.
event_link_v4.availability_clock: received_monotonic_ns within the capture host; received_wall_ns plus the causal retained server-time offset for cross-clock diagnostics
event_link_v4.event_time_audit: Replay must reproduce event_time_ms from exact wire and the typed row where available. Any mismatch fails the run.

typed_storage.physical_layout: The unchanged no-primary-key v3 typed tables are reused for v4 rows and are always filtered by the exact run ID.

terminal_report_contract.post_capture_materialization_exception_a: Persist or reconstruct a failed terminal report bound to the already terminal run; never retry or label it a startup failure.
terminal_report_contract.negative_corrected_latency_scope: All non-REST v4 event links with non-NULL provider event_time_ms.

non_claims: The v3 replay pass is infrastructure evidence only and does not qualify v3 or v4.
No model, edge, profitability, ROI, drawdown, leverage, paper, testnet, or live-trading authority is granted.
